summaryTemplate: {{className}} is the {{patternRole}}. It owns several subsystem objects and exposes a few high-level methods that perform the multi-step choreography internally. Pattern body lives at {{file}}:{{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}.
chosenRationaleTemplate: Tagged as Facade because {{className}} delegates to two or more distinct subsystem members (lines {{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}), wrapping their combined use behind one simpler interface.

facade_subsystem_member.note: A subsystem member. The facade owns it so callers never wire the subsystems together themselves.

facade_second_subsystem.note: A second subsystem member. Coordinating more than one subsystem is what distinguishes a facade from a plain wrapper.

runnerUpComparisonTemplates.Adapter: Adapter converts one object's interface to another. Facade simplifies the combined use of several subsystem objects — it is about reducing surface area, not converting a single interface.
